overview: Scaffold the monorepo, set up SQLite with FTS5, and ingest all 49 existing AEM DAM assets from .content.xml files into the database. This PRD must complete and be committed before the API and Frontend PRDs run in parallel worktrees.
goals: Scaffold Express backend + React Vite frontend in a single monorepo
Create SQLite schema with FTS5 full-text search and auto-sync triggers
Parse all 49 .content.xml files from Data/royal/ and load them into SQLite with deterministic enrichment
nonGoals: No API route implementation beyond GET /api/health
No React UI beyond the base scaffold
No AI enrichment in this PRD
successMetrics: npm run dev starts both Express (port 3001) and Vite (port 5173) without error
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM assets returns 49 after ingest

routes.name: Health Check
routes.purpose: Returns { status: 'ok' } — smoke test that server is running

uiNotes:
client/src/App.jsx renders a placeholder:

Royal Caribbean DAM
No styling required in this PRD — layout and branding come in the Frontend PRD

dataModel.description: Main table — original XML metadata plus deterministic enrichment fields
Full-text search index kept in sync with assets via triggers

rules: DATA_DIR env var points to the existing DAM export — default '../Data/royal' (relative to server/)
On server startup: run DB migrations, then run XML ingest only if SELECT COUNT(*) FROM assets = 0
Creator normalization: map '[Royal Carribean]', 'Brand Team', 'Brand Team ', '[Brand Team]', '[Production Team ]', '[Production Team]' → 'Royal Caribbean Brand Team'

Rights reconciliation: if xmpRights:Owner or dc:rights contains 'royal caribbean' (case-insensitive, trimmed) → enriched_rights_status='owned'; if rights data present but has typo/inconsistency → 'unclear'; if no rights data → 'none'

Channel inference from filename + dimensions: 'hero' in name → hero; 'banner' in name → banner; portrait (height > width) or 'mobile' in name → mobile; landscape (width > height) or 'desktop' in name → desktop; square → square
Format from dimensions: width > height → landscape; height > width → portrait; width == height → square
Release placeholder: any field value containing 'Selecione' → set has_release_placeholder = 1
S7 sync error: if dam:lastS7SyncStatus starts with 'failed' → set s7_sync_error = 1
JCR type stripping: '{Long}1920' → 1920 (integer), '{Boolean}true' → true, '{Date}2021-...' → string without wrapper
FTS sync triggers fire automatically on INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE to assets table
thumbnail_path and web_image_path are set only if those rendition files exist on disk

stories.title: Scaffold monorepo — Express + React Vite
stories.description: As a developer, I want a working monorepo with Express backend and React Vite frontend so I can build and test both apps from a single root.
acceptanceCriteria: Root package.json has workspaces: ['client', 'server'] and name: 'dam-search'
npm install concurrently at root

Root scripts: dev (concurrently runs server dev + client dev), test (cd server && npm test && cd ../client && npm test), lint (cd server && npm run lint && cd ../client && npm run lint), build (cd client && npm run build)
server/ package.json — install: npm install express better-sqlite3 multer cors dotenv openai uuid
server/ devDependencies — install: npm install --save-dev jest supertest nodemon eslint
server/ scripts: start (node src/index.js), dev (nodemon src/index.js), test (jest --runInBand --forceExit), lint (eslint src)

server/src/index.js: creates Express app, uses cors({ origin: 'http://localhost:5173' }), uses express.json(), mounts GET /api/health → res.json({ status: 'ok' }), listens on PORT env var defaulting to 3001, logs 'Server running on port 3001'
client/ created with: npm create vite@latest client -- --template react
client/ install: npm install axios
client/ devDependencies: npm install --save-dev vitest @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom @testing-library/user-event jsdom eslint
client/vite.config.js configures test: { environment: 'jsdom', globals: true, setupFiles: './src/test-setup.js' }
client/src/test-setup.js imports @testing-library/jest-dom
client/ scripts: dev (vite), build (vite build), test (vitest run), lint (eslint src)

client/src/App.jsx renders

Royal Caribbean DAM
.env.example at project root contains: OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key_here, PORT=3001, DATA_DIR=../Data/royal
.gitignore includes: node_modules/, dam.sqlite, uploads/, .env
uploads/ directory created with a .gitkeep file
Jest test: GET /api/health returns 200 { status: 'ok' }
Vitest test: App renders text 'Royal Caribbean DAM'
Example: npm run dev starts both servers without error
Negative case: missing OPENAI_API_KEY does not crash server — logs warning 'AI enrichment disabled: OPENAI_API_KEY not set' and continues

stories.title: SQLite schema, migrations, and FTS5 triggers
stories.description: As a developer, I want SQLite tables and FTS triggers created automatically on startup so the app has persistent searchable storage without manual setup.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/db/schema.js exports runMigrations(db)
runMigrations creates the assets table using CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS with all fields in the data model

runMigrations creates assets_fts: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS assets_fts USING fts5(id UNINDEXED, title, description, tags, location, creator, category, subcategory, filename, content='assets', content_rowid='rowid')
runMigrations creates three triggers: assets_ai (AFTER INSERT), assets_au (AFTER UPDATE), assets_ad (AFTER DELETE) to keep assets_fts in sync
server/src/db/index.js opens dam.sqlite at project root using better-sqlite3, calls runMigrations(db), exports db as default
server/src/index.js imports db on startup (triggering migrations)
Jest test: runMigrations on an in-memory DB (new Database(':memory:')) creates both tables without error
Jest test: INSERT a row into assets → SELECT from assets_fts WHERE assets_fts MATCH the inserted title returns that row
Jest test: UPDATE assets row title → FTS reflects new title
Jest test: DELETE assets row → FTS no longer returns it
Jest test: calling runMigrations twice does not throw
Negative case: if dam.sqlite path is not writable, process logs the error and exits with code 1

stories.title: XML ingest service — parse DAM export into SQLite
stories.description: As a developer, I want all 49 existing .content.xml files parsed and loaded into SQLite on first startup so assets are immediately available without a separate import step.
acceptanceCriteria: server/src/services/ingest.js exports ingestAssets(db, dataDir)
Recursively walks dataDir using Node fs/path, finds .content.xml files where: parent folder name ends in .jpg/.jpeg/.png/.JPG AND full path does not contain '_jcr_content'
Parses XML with Node's built-in DOMParser or the 'fast-xml-parser' package (npm install fast-xml-parser in server/) — reads all fields listed in dataModel

Namespace attribute mapping: dc:title → original_title, dc:description → original_description, dc:creator → original_creator, dc:rights → original_dc_rights, xmpRights:Owner → original_rights_owner, xmpRights:UsageTerms → original_usage_terms, Iptc4xmpExt:LocationShown → original_location, cq:tags → original_tags, tiff:ImageWidth → width, tiff:ImageLength → height, dam:size → file_size, dam:Fileformat → file_format, dam:MIMEtype → mime_type, dam:scene7File → scene7_file, dam:scene7Domain → scene7_domain, dam:scene7FileStatus → scene7_status
dam:lastS7SyncStatus is on the jcr:content element (not metadata child) — read it from there
Strip JCR type wrappers from all values: '{Long}1920' → 1920, '{Boolean}true' → 'true', '{Date}2021-...' → '2021-...'
Infer category/subcategory/subsubcategory from path segments relative to dataDir (last segment is the asset folder name itself — exclude it)
Apply all deterministic rules from the rules section: creator normalization, rights reconciliation, channel/format inference, release placeholder detection, s7 sync error flag
Set thumbnail_path to relative path of _jcr_content/renditions/cq5dam.thumbnail.319.319.png if it exists
Set web_image_path to relative path of _jcr_content/renditions/cq5dam.web.1280.1280.jpeg if it exists
Use INSERT OR IGNORE so re-running is safe
server/src/index.js: after DB init, if SELECT COUNT(*) FROM assets = 0, call ingestAssets(db, process.env.DATA_DIR || '../Data/royal') and log 'Ingested N assets'
Jest test: ingestAssets against the real ../Data/royal directory inserts exactly 49 rows
Jest test: asset with id 'anthem-of-the-seas-new-york-statue-liberty.jpg' has original_title 'Anthem of the Seas Sightseeing the Statue of Liberty, New York'
Jest test: creator '[Royal Carribean]' → enriched_creator_normalized = 'Royal Caribbean Brand Team'
Jest test: exactly 7 assets have has_release_placeholder = 1
Jest test: exactly 6 assets have s7_sync_error = 1
Jest test: exactly 27 assets have enriched_rights_status = 'none'
Jest test: asset 'anthem-of-the-seas-new-york-statue-liberty.jpg' has enriched_rights_status = 'owned'
Negative case: a malformed XML file is skipped with console.warn — remaining files still ingest successfully
